docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
[Replace this paragraph with your own bio. Write 3–4 sentences covering: your background and what you specialize in, the kinds of problems you work on, the tools and methods you use, and what you are currently looking for. Example below:]

I am a geospatial data scientist with a background in remote sensing and machine learning. I work on extracting actionable insights from satellite imagery and large spatial datasets using Python, Google Earth Engine, and open-source GIS tools. I am passionate about applying GeoAI techniques to real-world challenges in land use mapping, climate monitoring, and urban planning. I am currently seeking opportunities in [YOUR TARGET ROLE] in [YOUR TARGET LOCATION].

View My Projects Download CV

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• GIS & Remote Sensing

    • QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, Google Earth Engine
    • GDAL / OGR, GRASS GIS
    • Multispectral and SAR image analysis
    • Cloud Native Geospatial (COG, STAC, Zarr)

  • Programming

    • Python — GeoPandas, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2
    • JavaScript — Leaflet, MapLibre GL
    • SQL, PostgreSQL + PostGIS

  • Machine Learning & GeoAI

    • Supervised classification — Random Forest, XGBoost
    • Deep learning for image segmentation — U-Net, SAM
    • scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow
    • Object detection in satellite imagery

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

  • Drone / UAV Data Processing

    • Mission planning and flight operations
    • Photogrammetry: Agisoft Metashape, OpenDroneMap
    • Point cloud processing: CloudCompare, PDAL

docs.text:
I am open to work opportunities, collaborations, and project discussions. Feel free to reach out through any of the channels below.

Email your-email@example.com GitHub github.com/[YOUR-GITHUB-USERNAME] LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/[YOUR-LINKEDIN-USERNAME] ResearchGate researchgate.net/profile/[YOUR-PROFILE]

Download CV

docs.title: Senior Consulting Geophysicist — Ocean Floor Geophysics (Oceansight Group)
docs.text:
May 2025 – Present | Gibsons, B.C., Canada

  • Provide senior geophysical expertise within Ocean Floor Geophysics, now operating as a division of the Oceansight Group following acquisition of Ocean Floor Geophysics Inc.

docs.title: Founder & Senior Consulting Geophysicist — Ocean Floor Geophysics Inc.
docs.text:
January 2006 – May 2025 | Gibsons, B.C., Canada

  • Founded and led a company providing geophysical, AUV, and ROV services for deep-water mineral and gas hydrate exploration worldwide
  • Invented the OFG electromagnetic system (patent assigned); delivered EM, magnetic, gravity, and precision bathymetric mapping services using ROVs and AUVs
  • Served as chief scientist during acquisition of over 500 line-km of CSEM data in the Sea of Japan (2014); led two of the largest gas hydrate surveys ever conducted with the Scripps IOC Vulcan CSEM system (2015, 2017)

docs.title: Principal Geophysicist — Mira Geoscience, Advanced Geophysical Interpretation Centre (AGIC)
docs.text:
January 2006 – February 2018 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Led geophysical interpretation and project management with a focus on computerized inversions of geophysical data
  • Performed IP 2D/3D, 3D magnetic, and 1D/2D/3D electromagnetic inversions using UBC-GIF algorithms; inverted VTEM and ZTEM aeromagnetic datasets
  • Responsible for technical quality and sign-off on major projects; served as lead geophysicist on individual interpretation projects

docs.title: Consulting Geophysicist — Geoscience BC
docs.text:
July 2007 – July 2016 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Specified and technically supervised contracts for large, multi-year regional airborne gravity, electromagnetic, and magnetic data acquisition programs across British Columbia

docs.title: Chief Research Geophysicist — Barrick Gold
docs.text:
January 2005 – December 2005 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Coordinated the transfer of Placer Dome Inc. geophysical assets to Barrick Gold following Barrick's acquisition of PDI

docs.title: Chief Geophysicist — Placer Dome Inc.
docs.text:
May 1991 – January 2005 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Led worldwide minerals exploration and provided technical supervision of geophysicists and remote sensing specialists across PDI's 22 operating gold mines
  • Managed implementation of EM and electrical inversion algorithms; oversaw SWIR airborne hyperspectral scanner operations in the Kamchatka Peninsula, Russia
  • Provided consulting services for projects in Chile, Venezuela, and London (Placer Outokumpu); liased with university and government research groups

docs.title: Senior Geophysicist — Placer Dome Inc.
docs.text:
1987 – May 1991 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Conducted worldwide minerals exploration with technical supervision of exploration geophysics and remote sensing
  • Provided consulting geophysical services in Tanzania, Guyana, Fiji, and Chile
  • Managed specialized computing services including image processing and electrical modelling for PDI's exploration geophysics group

docs.title: Research Geophysicist — Placer Development Ltd. (Technical Services Group)
docs.text:
1981 – 1987 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Conducted minerals exploration and geophysical research worldwide, including gold exploration in Indonesia and Malaysia
  • Provided consulting services for PDUS in Nevada and Placer Exploration in Australia
  • Led research into image processing algorithms and coordinated image processing research across Placer Development and PEL

docs.title: Staff Geophysicist — Placer Development Ltd.
docs.text:
1978 – 1980 | Toronto, Ont., Canada

  • Explored for Archean/Precambrian gold, VMS polymetallic, and uranium deposits in eastern Canada; also worked on Carlin-style gold and caldera-hosted mercury/uranium programs in Nevada
  • Worked on the initial implementation of GEOLOG, PDI's computerized geologic logging system

docs.title: Consulting Geophysicist — Marcopper Mining Corp.
docs.text:
1975 – 1977 | Philippines

  • Conducted minerals exploration and computing consulting; developed theory and implemented work-index and recovery-sensitive cut-off grade definitions for mine grade control
  • Explored for porphyry copper and island-arc gold deposits throughout the Philippines

docs.title: Chief Geophysicist — Placer Exploration Pty. Ltd.
docs.text:
1973 – 1974 | Sydney, Australia

  • Led minerals exploration in Australasia, targeting Mississippi Valley-style and SEDEX lead-zinc deposits and porphyry copper and gold deposits in PNG and the Pacific islands

docs.title: Staff Geophysicist — Placer Development Ltd.
docs.text:
1970 – 1972 | Vancouver, B.C., Canada

  • Conducted field work and interpretation for copper porphyry, molybdenum porphyry, and skarn-style mineralization across the Western Cordillera

docs.title: B.Sc. in Geophysics
docs.text: University of British Columbia | 1970

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
